t: Persistent irritability + severe anger outbursts, 6 months; cannot be diagnosed separately from ODD.
Behavioral disturbance in the context of cognitive deficit.
Intimate partner violence suspicion - social service coordination.
Most patients improve with early parent training.
Comorbid ADHD, severe variant — risk of CD/ASPD.
Myth 1: "ODD is just ordinary 'stubborn child' behavior"
Clinical diagnosis based on functional impairment and frequency; distinct from normal life variant.
Severe punishment exacerbates symptoms; positive parenting training is evidence-based.
parent training first-line; antipsychotics rarely in refractory cases.
30% transition to CD; risk higher with childhood type and comorbidity; majority do not convert to CD.
Myth 5: "Only the child should be treated, not the family"
parent training is a central component; child psychotherapy alone is less effective.
Eyberg S.M. et al. Evidence-based psychosocial treatments for children and adolescents with disruptive behavior. J Clin Child Adolesc Psychol 2008;37(1):215–237.
